result.summary: PayPal exposes authenticated PayPal REST APIs, the Braintree GraphQL API, a remote MCP server at `https://mcp.paypal.com/mcp`, and a local MCP CLI package `@paypal/mcp`.
result.description: PayPal provides online payments, merchant checkout, invoicing, subscriptions, payouts, and related financial services. Its developer platform also includes Braintree payment infrastructure and AI-oriented tooling such as an MCP server.

paypal_app_client.label: PayPal app client ID and client secret
paypal_app_client.setup: In the [PayPal Developer Dashboard](https://developer.paypal.com/dashboard/), open **Apps & Credentials**. New accounts include a **Default Application**, or you can click **Create App** to make a new one. Copy the app's `client ID` and `client secret`. Use them to request an OAuth 2.0 access token from `https://api-m.sandbox.paypal.com/v1/oauth2/token` for sandbox or `https://api-m.paypal.com/v1/oauth2/token` for live.

paypal_access_token.label: PayPal OAuth 2.0 access token
paypal_access_token.setup: First get a PayPal app `client ID` and `client secret` from [Apps & Credentials](https://developer.paypal.com/dashboard/). Then exchange them for an access token by `POST`ing `grant_type=client_credentials` to `https://api-m.sandbox.paypal.com/v1/oauth2/token` (or the live host) using HTTP Basic auth with `client_id:client_secret`. Send the returned token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

braintree_access_token.label: Braintree GraphQL access token
braintree_access_token.setup: Create a [Braintree Sandbox account](https://www.braintreepayments.com/sandbox) or use your Braintree merchant account, then follow the GraphQL guide's auth flow to obtain an access token for Braintree Auth credentials. Use the token in the `Authorization` header for GraphQL requests.
